risk_if_not_implemented: Without Expeditious Disconnect / Disable Capability, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
compensating_control_1.description:
Automated mechanisms exist to enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for:
(1) Remote network access; 
(2) Third-party Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS); and/ or
(3) Non-console access to critical TAAS that store, transmit and/or process sensitive/regulated data.
compensating_control_1.justification: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) (IAC-06) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Expeditious Disconnect / Disable Capability (NET-14.8) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Remote Access
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to define, control and review organization-approved, secure remote access methods.
compensating_control_2.justification: Remote Access (NET-14) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Expeditious Disconnect / Disable Capability (NET-14.8) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
